questions.question: What is the key mindset shift when scaling from 50 to 500 customers?

options.text: From 'I do outreach' to 'my system generates pipeline'

questions.explanation: At 50 customers, you're a founder doing sales. At 500, you're an operator running an acquisition system. The shift is from personal execution to systematized pipeline generation.

questions.question: Why do tactics that produce 50 customers rarely produce 500?

options.text: Manual outreach and hustle don't scale — you need data-driven systems
Customers become harder to find after 50

questions.explanation: More manual outreach, more networking, more hustle won't produce 500 customers. Scaling requires a systems upgrade — CRM automation, analytics, AI-assisted outreach, and data-driven channel decisions.

questions.question: Before applying the Scaling playbook, which of these is a readiness requirement?

options.text: Knowing your current CAC and LTV

questions.explanation: Scale readiness requires knowing your CAC and LTV, having 50+ paying customers, at least one consistent acquisition channel, a CRM tracking pipeline stages, and a product with low churn. You can't optimize what you can't measure.

questions.question: The Scaling playbook is appropriate for founders who haven't yet reached 50 paying customers.
questions.explanation: False. Under 50 customers, use Lessons 2-5 (situation-specific playbooks). The Scaling playbook requires enough data and customer base to optimize systems and make data-driven channel decisions.

questions.question: Review the Scale Readiness checklist. Which items can you check off and which are missing? What needs to happen before you're ready for the Scaling playbook?
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response honestly assesses each readiness item (50+ customers, ICP clarity, proven channel, CRM tracking, CAC/LTV knowledge, low churn, time/budget availability), identifies missing prerequisites, and proposes a realistic plan to address gaps.
